text.title: AI-Feature Trust Metrics for Market Research Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-feature-trust-metrics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Trust scorecard MUST measure four dimensions: perceived accuracy, transparency, recovery, fairness.

rationale.source: Nielsen Norman 2026 AI trust research; PAIR design guidelines

rule.rationale: These four cover the dominant trust dimensions in 2025-2026 AI-UX literature; trimming below four loses coverage.

rule.statement: Each dimension MUST have at least one instrumented signal (event), not only survey items.
rule.rationale: Survey-only trust metrics lag by weeks and survey fatigue inflates noise; in-product signals close the loop.

rule.statement: Baseline survey + signal capture MUST be done before any rollout >5% of users.
rule.rationale: Without a baseline, post-launch movements are unattributable.

rule.statement: Fairness metric MUST be reported split by ≥2 user segments (language, region, plan tier).
rule.rationale: Single-population fairness hides disparate impact; cuts surface it.

rule.statement: If WAU < 100 OR no instrumentation pipeline exists, skip and run 1-1 trust interviews.
rule.rationale: Below thresholds the signals are too noisy to be actionable.
